payload.title: Wrapper hard-links source into cache → can truncate originals
payload.description:
trust-rustc's mirror_module_tree_with_extras (crates/trust-rustc/src/lib.rs) hard-links NON-strict source files into the /tmp/trust-cache mirror via fs::hard_link(&path, &dest). A hard link shares the inode, so a later write/truncate of the cached copy corrupts the ORIGINAL source file.

Observed during RT-70: under cache-clear + cargo clean + wrapper-rebuild churn, crates/trust-rustc/src/lib.rs and crates/xtask/src/std_signatures.rs were both truncated to 0 bytes in the working tree (restored from git). Both are non-strict child files of strict multi-file crates, hard-linked into the cache.

Fix: use fs::copy (not hard_link) for the cache mirror, or only ever write via tmp+rename and never hard-link source. Add a regression test: mutate a cached file, assert the source is untouched.
